extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR won Stage 17 atop Col du Portet, extending his yellow jersey lead to 5:39 over VINGEGAARD after dropping rivals on the beyond-category final climb.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT positioned for KOM points grab late in Tour, targeting 50-point mountain stages despite sprinter profile.
KELDERMAN over 8 minutes back signals UAE can ignore him, freeing domestiques for alternative race strategies.
CARAPAZ big attack caused VINGEGAARD to suffer hard, regaining 200m with sprint rather than sustained climbing power.
PEREZ survived 4-minute gap over second climb in breakaway, suggesting early escape depth matters more than pure wattage.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Extended GC lead to 5:39, ninth yellow jersey day, demonstrating uncrackable form on beyond-category finishes.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Suffered hard after CARAPAZ attack but recovered to second place, showing resilience but not attacking capacity.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Big attack caused VINGEGAARD distress and moved into third GC, indicating form building late in Tour.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Late-Tour pivot to KOM competition reveals tactical flexibility, targeting specific point-heavy stages.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: Over 8 minutes down signals complete GC collapse, no longer merits marking by contenders.
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: Third consecutive polka dot day with only 11-point cushion, vulnerable but consistently finding breakaway opportunities.
rider_signals.PEREZ Anthony: positive: Bastille Day breakaway success after surviving 4-minute deficit over penultimate climb shows exceptional durability.
